name: Embalse de Tous — Return from Alberic via Camí de la Taronja
name_original: El Embalse de Tous — Vuelta desde Alberic por el Camí de la Taronja
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Alberic, located in Valencia, Spain, offer a diverse landscape for outdoor activities. The region features tranquil agricultural routes, scenic river paths along the Júcar River, and converted old railway lines known as Vías Verdes. These paths often present minimal elevation changes, making them suitable for various cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Alberic The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de Tous — Return from Alberic via Camí de la Taronja, a 26.5 miles (42.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 12 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views towards the Embalse de Tous. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Bolbaite natural pool – Bridge over the Sellent River loop from Alberic, a difficult 43.1 miles (69.4 km) path. This route explores the natural surroundings, including a bridge over the Sellent River. Local touring cyclists also love the Views from the Tous Pass – View of Embalse de Tous loop from Alberic, a 18.4 miles (29.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain with vistas of the Embalse de Tous, often completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Touring cycling around Alberic is defined by extensive orange groves, tranquil river paths, and flat Vías Verdes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy agricultural routes to more challenging paths with moderate elevation. The routes in Alberic are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alberic's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Alberic?
A: There are 15 touring cycling routes around Alberic, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 2 easy routes, 10 moderate routes, and 3 more challenging paths, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Alberic?
A: Alberic's touring cycling routes feature a mix of landscapes. You'll find tranquil agricultural paths, especially through extensive orange groves, scenic river paths along the Júcar River, and converted old railway lines known as Vías Verdes. These Vías Verdes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3229067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Trail of the Old Little Train&lt;/a&gt;, are typically flat and traffic-free, ideal for relaxed rides. While many routes have minimal elevation changes, some paths venture into more varied terrain with moderate climbs.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of significant natural and historical landmarks. You can explore areas like the municipal natural site of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6171147" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Salines&lt;/a&gt;, which was once an old salt mine. Other routes provide scenic vistas of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6282584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Assut d’Antella&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2781607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bitter Spring&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter historical structures such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3331792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alzira Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6282611" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gavarda Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or families in Alberic?
A: Yes, Alberic offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families looking for a relaxed ride. These often follow flat Vías Verdes or agricultural paths through orange groves, providing a gentle cycling experience with minimal elevation. For example, routes exploring the extensive orange groves along the Júcar River are generally easy and scenic.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alberic?
A: The Valencia region, including Alberic, is generally pleasant for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer ideal conditions with mild temperatures and less intense sun, making it comfortable for longer rides. Winter can also be a good option, especially for those escaping colder climates, as the weather remains relatively mild and sunny. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended if cycling during this season.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Alberic area?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10352088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from the Tous Pass – View of Embalse de Tous loop from Alberic&lt;/a&gt; offers varied terrain and vistas. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38260420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolbaite natural pool – Bridge over the Sellent River loop from Alberic&lt;/a&gt;, which explores natural surroundings.

Q: What are the typical durations for touring cycling routes around Alberic?
A: The duration of touring cycling routes in Alberic varies depending on the distance and difficulty. Shorter, moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10352088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from the Tous Pass – View of Embalse de Tous loop from Alberic&lt;/a&gt; (18.4 miles / 29.6 km) can be completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Longer, more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38260420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolbaite natural pool – Bridge over the Sellent River loop from Alberic&lt;/a&gt; (43.1 miles / 69.4 km), may take around 4 hours 21 minutes.

Q: Are there options for longer, multi-day touring cycling adventures from Alberic?
A: While Alberic itself offers day routes, the region is connected to broader cycling networks that facilitate longer adventures. The renowned &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/V%C3%ADa_Verde_de_Ojos_Negros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de Ojos Negros&lt;/a&gt;, though not directly through Alberic, is an extensive greenway connecting inland Teruel to the coast of Valencia. This route offers diverse landscapes and can be incorporated into multi-day touring plans for those seeking a more extended journey.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Alberic?
A: The touring cycling routes around Alberic are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the tranquil agricultural routes through orange groves, the scenic river paths, and the well-maintained Vías Verdes that offer a relaxed and picturesque cycling experience.

Q: Is parking available near the starting points of touring cycling routes in Alberic?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Alberic, particularly in the town center or near popular access points for the Vías Verdes and other cycling routes. It's advisable to check local signage for any restrictions or designated parking areas, especially when starting a ride from a specific trailhead or village.

Q: Can I reach touring cycling routes in Alberic using public transport?
A: Alberic is connected by public transport, including train services, which can be a convenient way to reach the area. Many routes start directly from the town or are easily accessible from the town center, making it feasible to arrive by public transport and begin your cycling adventure. It's recommended to check the specific public transport provider's policies regarding bicycles during peak hours.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking more of a challenge, Alberic offers routes with greater distances and elevation changes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38260420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolbaite natural pool – Bridge over the Sellent River loop from Alberic&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult, covering 43.1 miles (69.4 km) with significant elevation gain, exploring more rugged natural surroundings.
tips: Getting to the port of Tous requires some effort, although the slopes are quite gentle and you can do it quietly enjoying the scenery. Once you arrive at the parking lot of the dam, there is nothing left to do but enjoy beautiful views of the "swamp"

This site offers very nice views of the Tous reservoir. It is a pity that you cannot cross over the dam itself, which would allow you to reach the other side and be able to connect with the towns of La Canal de Navarres. Getting here is very pleasant and you enjoy it a lot, especially the tranquility of the place.

The Antella dam offers wonderful views of the Júcar river and the valley around it. There is a park with a lot of shade, ideal for the summer. A few meters away is the pier of the canoeing club, a very quiet and picturesque place that you cannot miss.

waypoints.name: View of the Júcar River at the Antella Weir
